8:["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold","children":"Pricing Strategy That Works"}]
9:["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] leading-relaxed mb-4","children":["The core of making money in Supermarket Simulator is markup discipline. Every product has a wholesale cost and a retail price you set. The community consensus from Reddit and Steam forums is that a ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"1.25x to 1.5x markup"}]," works best for most items. Go higher and you risk driving customers away; go lower and you leave profit on the table."]}]
a:["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] leading-relaxed mb-4","children":"Luxury and electronics can handle higher markups (1.8x to 2.2x) because customers expect to pay more. Essentials like bread, milk, and eggs should stay closer to 1.2x — customers are price-sensitive on daily necessities. Here is a detailed breakdown based on community-tested data:"}]

c:["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#d97706] font-semibold mb-2","children":"Pro Tip"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"Use the pricing gun to batch-update prices during slow hours (early morning, 6-8 AM in-game). Changing prices while customers are shopping causes frustration and lost sales. A single price change takes 2-3 seconds per shelf — plan accordingly."}]]}]
d:["$","section",null,{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-3 mb-4","children":[["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":[["path",{"d":"M16 7h6v6","key":"box55l"}],["path",{"d":"m22 7-8.5 8.5-5-5L2 17","key":"1t1m79"}]],"className":"lucide-trending-up w-6 h-6 text-[#d97706]"}],["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold","children":"High-Profit Product Categories"}]]}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] leading-relaxed mb-4","children":"Not all products are created equal. Some categories deliver far more profit per unit or per shelf slot than others. Here is a detailed breakdown of the best moneymakers, ranked by community-tested profitability:"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"space-y-4","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#d97706] mb-1","children":"Electronics & Appliances"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm mb-2","children":"Items like headphones, phone chargers, and small appliances have high wholesale costs but even higher retail margins. They sell slower (1-2 units per day per shelf), but each sale brings significant profit. A single headphone sale can yield $15-$25 profit."}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-xs","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Best for:"}]," Late-game stores with 12+ shelves. Limit to 1-2 shelves early on."]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#d97706] mb-1","children":"Alcohol & Tobacco"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm mb-2","children":"These require a license purchase ($500-$800 for alcohol, $300-$500 for tobacco), but the profit per unit is among the highest in the game. A bottle of wine costing $8 wholesale sells for $14-$16 retail — nearly 100% profit. The license pays for itself within 3-5 in-game days if you dedicate 1-2 shelves."}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-xs","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Best for:"}]," Mid-game stores with steady $500+ daily profit. Priority unlock after freezer."]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#d97706] mb-1","children":"Snacks & Soft Drinks"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm mb-2","children":"Low margin per item (typically $0.30-$0.80 profit), but extremely high turnover. A single snack shelf can sell 15-25 units per day. These are your volume drivers. Keep them fully stocked at all times — empty snack shelves are lost money."}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-xs","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Best for:"}]," Every store, every stage. Dedicate 2-3 shelves minimum."]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#d97706] mb-1","children":"Frozen Foods"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm mb-2","children":"Require freezer units (upfront investment of $800-$1,200), but frozen pizzas and ice cream have solid margins ($2-$4 profit per unit) and steady demand. A freezer shelf typically sells 8-12 units per day. Buy the freezer early if you have the capital — it unlocks a high-margin category competitors often skip."}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-xs","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Best for:"}]," Early-to-mid game. Buy freezer after adding 4-6 regular shelves."]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#d97706] mb-1","children":"Energy Drinks"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm mb-2","children":"A niche category with surprisingly high margins. Energy drinks cost $1.50-$2.00 wholesale and sell for $3.00-$4.00 retail. Turnover is moderate (5-10 units/day), making them a solid middle-ground between snacks and electronics."}],"$L19"]}]]}]]}]
e:["$","section",null,{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-3 mb-4","children":[["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":[["path",{"d":"M8 2v4","key":"1cmpym"}],["path",{"d":"M16 2v4","key":"4m81vk"}],["rect",{"width":"18","height":"18","x":"3","y":"4","rx":"2","key":"1hopcy"}],["path",{"d":"M3 10h18","key":"8toen8"}]],"className":"lucide-calendar w-6 h-6 text-[#d97706]"}],["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold","children":"Promotional Timing: When to Raise, When to Lower"}]]}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] leading-relaxed mb-4","children":"The game features dynamic customer traffic patterns. Weekends and in-game holidays bring more shoppers. Lower prices slightly (10-15% discount) before high-traffic periods to drive volume. The increased foot traffic more than compensates for the lower margin."}],["$","div",null,{"className":"overflow-x-auto mb-4","children":["$","table",null,{"className":"w-full text-sm border 10-15%"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"p-3","children":"+30-50% customer count, higher total revenue"}]]}],["$","tr",null,{"className":"border-b border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","td",null,{"className":"p-3","children":"Rainy Day"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"p-3","children":"Raise prices 5-10%"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"p-3","children":"Customers less price-sensitive, higher margin"}]]}],["$","tr",null,{"className":"border-b border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","td",null,{"className":"p-3","children":"Slow Weekday"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"p-3","children":"Keep standard prices"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"p-3","children":"Stable baseline, no risk of driving away few customers"}]]}],["$","tr",null,{"children":[["$","td",null,{"className":"p-3","children":"End-of-Day Clearance"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"p-3","children":"Discount perishables 20-30%"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"p-3","children":"Sell before spoilage, recover wholesale cost"}]]}]]}]]}]}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] leading-relaxed","children":"Conversely, raise prices slightly on rainy days when customers are less price-sensitive and more focused on convenience. Small adjustments add up over time. A 5% price increase on a $1,000 daily revenue store adds $50/day — enough to cover a cashier's wage."}]]}]
f:["$","section",null,{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-3 mb-4","children":[["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":[["path",{"d":"M11 21.73a2 2 0 0 0 2 0l7-4A2 2 0 0 0 21 16V8a2 2 0 0 0-1-1.73l-7-4a2 2 0 0 0-2 0l-7 4A2 2 0 0 0 3 8v8a2 2 0 0 0 1 1.73z","key":"1a0edw"}],["path",{"d":"M12 22V12","key":"d0xqtd"}],["polyline",{"points":"3.29 7 12 12 20.71 7","key":"ousv84"}],["path",{"d":"m7.5 4.27 9 5.15","key":"1c824w"}]],"className":"lucide-package w-6 h-6 text-[#d97706]"}],["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold","children":"Minimize Waste: The Silent Profit Killer"}]]}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] leading-relaxed mb-4","children":"Over-ordering perishable goods (produce, dairy, baked goods) leads to spoilage and direct losses. Spoiled inventory is a 100% loss — you paid wholesale and recover $0. Here is how to keep waste under control:"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"space-y-4","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#d97706] mb-1","children":"Step 1: Order Conservatively"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"For perishables, order no more than 1.5x your daily sell-through rate. If you sell 10 units of milk per day, order 15 units maximum. This gives you a small buffer without risking spoilage."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#d97706] mb-1","children":"Step 2: Check Both Suppliers"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"The local market often has better deals than the online supplier for perishables — especially produce and baked goods. Check both before placing large orders. Local market prices can be 10-20% lower with no shipping fee."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#d97706] mb-1","children":"Step 3: Discount Before Spoilage"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"If you notice perishables approaching their expiration date, discount them 20-30% to move inventory fast. Recovering 70% of your cost is far better than losing 100% to spoilage."}]]}]]}]]}]
10:["$","section",null,{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-3 mb-4","children":[["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":[["path",{"d":"m21.73 18-8-14a2 2 0 0 0-3.48 0l-8 14A2 2 0 0 0 4 21h16a2 2 0 0 0 1.73-3","key":"wmoenq"}],["path",{"d":"M12 9v4","key":"juzpu7"}],["path",{"d":"M12 17h.01","key":"p32p05"}]],"className":"lucide-triangle-alert w-6 h-6 text-[#d97706]"}],["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold","children":"Common Money-Making Mistakes"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"space-y-4","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-red-50 border border-red-200","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-red-700 mb-1","children":"Mistake #1: Chasing High Margin on Low Turnover"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"Filling your store with electronics (2.0x markup) sounds great, but if each shelf sells 1 unit per day, your total profit is lower than a snack shelf selling 20 units at 1.3x markup. Balance high-margin and high-turnover categories."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-red-50 border border-red-200","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-red-700 mb-1","children":"Mistake #2: Ignoring Shipping Costs"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"Online orders have shipping fees that eat into margins. A $50 order with a $8 shipping fee reduces your effective margin by 16%. For small orders, the local market is often cheaper even at slightly higher per-unit prices."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-red-50 border border-red-200","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-red-700 mb-1","children":"Mistake #3: Never Adjusting Prices"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"Setting prices once and forgetting them is leaving money on the table. Review prices every 3-5 in-game days based on customer feedback and sell-through rates. A 10% price increase on your top 5 products can boost daily profit by $100+."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-red-50 border border-red-200","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-red-700 mb-1","children":"Mistake #4: Buying Licenses Too Late"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"Alcohol and tobacco licenses cost $800-$1,300 total but generate $200-$400 extra daily profit once active. Delaying the license purchase by 5 days means losing $1,000-$2,000 in potential revenue. Buy them as soon as you have $1,500 in reserve."}]]}]]}]]}]
11:["$","section",null,{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-3 mb-4","children":[["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":[["circle",{"cx":"12","cy":"12","r":"10","key":"1mglay"}],["path",{"d":"M9.09 9a3 3 0 0 1 5.83 1c0 2-3 3-3 3","key":"1u773s"}],["path",{"d":"M12 17h.01","key":"p32p05"}]],"className":"lucide-circle-question-mark w-6 h-6 text-[#d97706]"}],["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold","children":"Frequently Asked Questions"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"space-y-4","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#181c25] mb-1","children":"Q: What is the fastest way to make money early game?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"A: Focus on snacks and soft drinks with 1.3x-1.5x markup. They have high turnover and require no special equipment or licenses. A single snack shelf can generate $10-$20 daily profit with minimal investment."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#181c25] mb-1","children":"Q: Should I buy the freezer or alcohol license first?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"A: Buy the freezer first ($800-$1,200). Frozen foods provide steady daily profit with no ongoing license fees. The alcohol license is next priority once you have $1,500+ in reserve after the freezer purchase."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#181c25] mb-1","children":"Q: How much daily profit do I need before expanding?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"A: Wait until you have $3,000+ in cash reserves and consistent $800+ daily profit. Floor expansions cost $2,000-$5,000, and new shelves need stock. Expanding too early leaves you cash-strapped and unable to fill new space."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#181c25] mb-1","children":"Q: Do discounts actually increase total profit?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"A: Yes, during high-traffic periods. A 10% discount that brings 30% more customers typically increases total revenue by 15-20%. The key is timing — only discount when you know foot traffic will be high (weekends, holidays)."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#181c25] mb-1","children":"Q: What is the most profitable store size?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"A: Community data suggests the sweet spot is 12-16 shelves with 3-4 employees. Beyond 20 shelves, management overhead (restocking, pricing, staff supervision) becomes so time-consuming that profit per minute of playtime starts to decline."}]]}]]}]]}]
